final_artifact:
Subject: The other side of the Transformers code agent policy

Hi Ilyas — saw your PR #45634 land under the new Code Agent Policy banner in the Transformers template. The "we're bottlenecked on review capacity" framing is striking, especially since you've been pretty open about using agents thoughtfully yourself ("needed both claude and copilot's help on this one 😅" on the vision/audio tensor extraction PR was a nice example of transparent provenance).

That's the tension we're trying to help with at Entire. We built an open-source CLI (Checkpoints) that captures Claude Code / Cursor / Copilot sessions on git push and links the prompts + transcripts to the resulting commits — so a reviewer can tell a thoughtful agent-assisted PR from a pure OpenClaw drive-by in about 10 seconds.

Runs locally, free, no account. If it'd be useful signal for the HF side of the policy conversation, happy to share what we've seen. Otherwise, ignore freely.

— [NAME], Entire

content.text: I'll research this carefully. The pain moment is interesting — IlyasMoutawwakil is the PR author, but the Code Agent Policy is in the *template* itself, meaning it was added by HF maintainers. Let me check the actual context.

Great context. Ilyas is a HuggingFace engineer (works on Intel XPU, optimum-intel, kernels, FP8 quantization). His PR #45396 explicitly says "needed both claude and copilot's help on this one 😅" — he's a thoughtful AI tooling user himself, not anti-agent. He's doing legitimate, deep technical work (vision encoders, torch.compile, FP8 kernels) and using AI agents in a way that's transparent about provenance.

This is the perfect angle: he's *not* the problem — he's the person whose good-faith AI-assisted PRs are getting harder to land because the repo is drowning in low-signal agent PRs. Entire is exactly the kind of provenance/intent layer that would distinguish his work from spam.

Let me draft.

**Draft:**

Runs locally, free, no account. If it'd be useful signal for the HF side of the policy conversation, happy to share what we've seen. Otherwise, ignore freely.

— [NAME], Entire

content.text: Critique didn't return substantive issues. The draft is tight, specific, references his actual PR #45396 with the exact quote, picks up "OpenClaw" from the policy text, and respects all constraints (word count ~145, peer-to-peer, no demo pitch, low-stakes offer). Submitting.
